hard.next_step: June 17 Rick: Make the first pass shortlist in 6 weeks, present Demo, then make the final 2 list in 8 additional weeks.
hard.ais_why:
• The buyer made AI the headline driver on the May 8 2026 intro call. The Ashley team told Zycus that "Upper management's primary driver is AI", with the initial focus on AI-driven spend analytics, supplier onboarding, eSourcing and risk. This is buyer-stated language captured live in a recorded discovery, not vendor positioning.
• The scope explicitly includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI and ANA, and Zycus has rated AI importance as 'Very Critical' - the highest tier. The buyer corroborated by asking about Teams-based Merlin Intake ("Anybody use Teams?") and probing system accuracy and flexibility for AI-driven categorization.
• Ashley is driving toward an RFP with a tight 6-to-8-week shortlist and a mid-2027 go-live, requesting demo materials and bid documents the same day. The reaction to the AI demos was strongly positive and the RFP submission is committed, indicating budget and urgency are aligned with the AI agenda.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live AI-first S2P RFP in Formal Evaluation with a strong 8 May demo reaction, but the buyer has gone quiet since 27 May and never returned the user counts Zycus needs to price, so the shortlist decision is drifting.
north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 81 (close 2026-09-25, today 2026-07-06). Created 30 Apr 2026, qualified 1 May, moved Qualified -> Formal Evaluation 11 May; ~56 days in Formal Evaluation with no forward stage progression. Buyer's own plan (Avoma 8 May, Ashley Frantz): decision narrowed to final two in 6-8 weeks then a single vendor, go-live June 2027. Zycus submitted the Phase 1 RFP response 27 May but flagged unanswered commercial questions and missing user counts; last buyer touch was 27 May (40 days ago). Rep updated Next Step 17 Jun (rep-side, awaiting reply). The 25 Sep close date lands inside the buyer's final-vendor window with zero room for proposal, negotiation, MSA/SOW, legal or InfoSec, so a realistic close is Nov-Dec 2026; the date needs a 45-60 day push.
evidence: Buyer set the pace on the 8 May intro call: Ashley Frantz said 'we're definitely looking from a decision standpoint to having a decision within the next 6 to 8 weeks... narrow down to the final two' (source: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026)

Zycus submitted its RFP response on 27 May but with open commercial gaps: 'we have not yet received responses to our outstanding questions, including several commercial re...' and could not price without user counts (source: SF Task 27 May 2026, Clari email sent)
Zycus chased the user-count spreadsheet twice (15 May request, 26 May chase) with no buyer reply, blocking pricing (source: SF Tasks 26 May 2026)

Last verified buyer engagement was 27 May 2026 (40 days ago); Next Step updated by Rick 17 Jun is a rep-side touch awaiting reply, not two-way engagement (source: SF LastActivityDate 27 May, Next Step 17 Jun 2026)

Strong AI fit corroborated live: Courtney Kessel said 'the biggest push from management and upper management has been AI... emphasis on the AI', matching the AIS 'Strong' (8.0) rating (source: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026)

metrics.narrative: The buyer has a clear strategic driver but no quantified value case yet. On the 8 May intro call Courtney Kessel said 'the biggest push from management and upper management has been AI... heavily on AI, but more so spend analytics, supplier onboarding, and some eSourcing... supplier risk, resiliency, and performance', and Tim Adams framed the operational pain as 'a lot of manual intervention, and we know there's better ways... devoting some time under the AI umbrella to modernize our daily execution and provide some efficiency'. No hard numbers were shared (annual spend under management, target savings %, headcount, cycle-time), and when Ronit showed Spendminer savings, Ashley Frantz probed the accuracy ('does it factor a time frame for lowest unit price... not something 12 months ago'), which is value-case scrutiny without a stated target. The move is to quantify the Phase 1 business case (spend visibility, sourcing savings, touchless risk) with Ashley's finance/leadership before the shortlist so the AI mandate has an ROI number attached.
sources: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026 (Courtney Kessel, Tim Adams, Ashley Frantz)

economic_buyer.narrative: No individual economic buyer is named in Salesforce contact roles or in the call. The budget authority sits with unnamed 'upper management': Courtney Kessel said the AI push comes 'from management and upper management', and Ashley Frantz confirmed the path is 'we'll go through executive approvals as any company normally would... a lot of leadership engaged in this process' and that the group on the call is 'the steering committee on this decision'. The buyer also confirmed this is 'a budgeted project'. So budget exists and an executive sponsor exists at leadership level, but no name or title has been exposed and no executive has joined a call. The move is to ask Ashley Frantz or Matthew Roby to name the executive sponsor and secure that person into a value/proposal review before the final-two decision.
sources: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026 (Courtney Kessel, Ashley Frantz)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are partly explicit from the call and partly in the Phase 1 RFP. Ashley Frantz laid out the functional must-haves: 'spend analytics and category management... supplier onboarding and supplier management for both direct and indirect... an eSourcing tool... supplier risk on the direct material side... catalog or guided buying' with punch-out, and defined a hard boundary: 'contracts or contract life cycle management... is definitely out of scope, legal still wants to keep their tool'. AI is the overriding weighting ('emphasis on the AI'), plus integration fit with AS/400 (ERP) and Maximo (must continue for work-order demand, not for purchasing). Accuracy and configurability surfaced as criteria too, via Ashley's questions on classification accuracy and her concern about AI configurability causing downstream breakage. These are confirmed asks; how they are scored in the formal Phase 1 RFP is not yet visible. The move is to map each RFP scoring criterion to a Merlin/Agentic proof point in the demo.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured, self-paced RFP with a defined committee and a fast clock. Ashley Frantz described the process: 'a decision within the next 6 to 8 weeks... narrow down to the final two... then go through executive approvals... proper sign offs'. The committee present on 8 May was procurement-side (Courtney Kessel indirect lead, Ashley Frantz indirect director, plus direct-side leaders Paul Ringo and Tim Adams); Matthew Roby (Senior Director, Corporate Procurement) was named as committee but 'couldn't be on the call today'. The process is phased by design: Phase 1 (AI spend analytics, sourcing, supplier onboarding, risk) then Phase 2 (full indirect procure-to-pay out of Maximo). The gaps in the process map are the executive-approval layer (unnamed) and whether Zycus is confirmed onto the shortlist. The move is to confirm shortlist status and the named approver chain in the next buyer conversation.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in view, which is normal for an early Formal-Evaluation RFP. The only paper touched so far is the mutual NDA: an MNDA was requested internally by Ashley Frantz (4 May), signed by Zycus (Rick, 15 May) after portal/one-click-login access friction was worked around with Zycus's own e-signature. Ashley Frantz confirmed CLM is out of scope because legal keeps its own tool (Intellidree), so the eventual paper will center on the S2P platform Order Form, SOW and MSA rather than contract-management modules. Implementation would be Zycus-delivered, not a third-party SI ('we have an implementation team... led by a VP... one throat to choke'). Ronit floated a phased implementation (upstream sourcing first, catalogs in Phase 2) and offered case studies. No procurement/legal/InfoSec channel has been named. Visibility comes from asking, at shortlist, who owns contracting and InfoSec and what the buyer's paper standard is.
sources: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026 (Ashley Frantz, Ronit, Rick)

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is confirmed and articulated by the buyer. Tim Adams named it directly for the direct side: 'a lot of manual intervention, and we know there's better ways... modernize our daily execution and provide some efficiency', with spend analytics and sourcing as the primary pain areas. Ashley Frantz named the indirect pain: no modern spend analytics/category management, manual supplier onboarding and risk, third-party punch-out catalogs, and a longer-term need to move indirect purchasing out of Maximo entirely (requisitions, approvals, PO, 3-way match, invoicing, AP). The over-arching pain is a top-down AI modernization mandate ('the biggest push from upper management has been AI'). Pain owners are the procurement leaders (Frantz indirect, Adams/Ringo direct) with the executive sponsor above them. This is confirmed pain; the missing piece is the quantified cost of that pain (see Metrics).
sources: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026 (Tim Adams, Ashley Frantz, Courtney Kessel)

champion.narrative: No confirmed champion yet, but a developing candidate. On the 8 May call Ashley Frantz (Director of Indirect Purchasing) was the most active buyer voice: she defined the scope, the boundaries (CLM out), the integration constraints (AS/400, Maximo), the timeline, and pushed hard test questions on accuracy and AI configurability, which signals genuine ownership of the evaluation. She is the strongest champion path. Matthew Roby (Senior Director, Corporate Procurement) is the most senior procurement contact and named to the steering committee, but he missed the call and has shown no advocacy behavior. Courtney Kessel (Purchasing Category Leader) invited Zycus into the RFP after the conference (a warm origin) but was less vocal on scope. None has yet introduced the executive sponsor, coached Zycus on competitors, or pushed the process forward, and the buyer has been silent for 40 days, so the candidate is at risk of going transactional. The move is to re-engage Ashley Frantz directly, convert her into an advocate by helping her build the internal AI business case, and get the executive-sponsor introduction.
sources: SF Task 8 May 2026 (Kessel invited Zycus into RFP)

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in any source: Salesforce Competitors__c is empty, and no rival was mentioned on the 8 May call. The buyer confirmed this is a genuine multi-vendor RFP (Ashley Frantz: 'narrow down to the final two... if not a single vendor within 6 to 8 weeks'), so competition exists but is undisclosed, and none can be asserted without inventing one. The one competitive anecdote on the call was Zycus's own reference story about winning PwC away from a Coupa-implemented incumbent (Jaggaer) via an 800-question AI-answered RFP, which is Zycus positioning, not a rival in this deal. Zycus's wedge if a rival surfaces is the AI-first stack the buyer explicitly asked for: Merlin Intake in Teams, Agentic AI (autonomous negotiation, AP Smartdesk), 100% touchless Supplier Risk Radar, and patented Auto Class spend classification. The move is to extract the shortlist and named rivals from Ashley Frantz or Courtney Kessel during the shortlist phase so the demo can be tailored.

deal_movement.summary: Deal moved fast early (created 30 Apr, Amount lifted 120k->320k same day, qualified 1 May, Formal Evaluation 11 May, CloseDate pulled in from 2027 to 25 Sep 2026 on 12 May) then stalled: the 8 May demo landed well, the Phase 1 RFP came in 8 May, Zycus responded 27 May with open commercial gaps, and the buyer has been silent for 40 days. 3 Jul the deal was re-tagged Division Zycus -> New Markets.

items.change: Amount raised from $120K to $320K as full S2P scope was set
CloseDate pulled forward from 16 Sep 2027 to 25 Sep 2026
8 May intro demo delivered; buyer reaction strongly positive on AI
Phase 1 RFP received from Ashley Furniture
Zycus submitted RFP response but flagged unanswered commercial questions and missing user counts (could not price)

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is named in any evidence to date (Competitors__c empty; none mentioned on the 8 May call), yet the buyer confirmed a real multi-vendor RFP driving to a final two in 6-8 weeks, so rivals exist but are undisclosed. No rival can be listed without fabrication, and none is buyer-preferred on the evidence. The single most important competitive action is not ranking a known field but discovering it: extract the shortlist and named vendors from Ashley Frantz or Courtney Kessel before the demo. Zycus's differentiator against any S2P peer is the AI-first stack the buyer explicitly demanded (Merlin Intake in Teams, Agentic AI negotiation and AP Smartdesk, touchless Supplier Risk Radar, patented Auto Class spend classification), which maps directly to upper management's AI mandate.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional and AI fit on the buyer's stated Phase 1 needs; the exposed edges are integration depth (AS/400, Maximo) and the buyer's caution about AI configurability sprawl.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage AI-led RFP with a strong demo but thinning engagement; the win levers are re-igniting the buyer, converting Ashley Frantz to a champion, and reaching the executive AI sponsor.
flags: Re-engage now: buyer silent 40 days and the first-pass shortlist is due, unblock by answering their open commercial questions and asking for the user counts to price.
Convert Ashley Frantz into a champion: she owns the evaluation and set the scope, help her build the internal AI business case and get her to name the executive sponsor.
Multi-thread beyond procurement: only mid-level procurement is engaged, reach the direct-side leaders (Paul Ringo, Tim Adams) and the unnamed executive AI sponsor before the final-two cut.

champion_strength.summary: No confirmed champion. Ashley Frantz is the strongest developing candidate: she owns and drove the evaluation on 8 May (scope, boundaries, integration, timeline, tough accuracy/AI-governance questions), which is genuine ownership, but she has not yet advocated for Zycus, opened the executive sponsor, or coached on competitors, and the buyer has been silent 40 days. Matthew Roby, the more senior procurement contact, has shown no engagement. Until Frantz introduces the AI executive sponsor and shares evaluation intel, the deal is single-threaded to mid-level indirect procurement.
champion_strength.source: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026; SF OpportunityContactRole

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus is well-positioned on AI: the 8 May demo led with Merlin Intake in Teams, Agentic AI (autonomous negotiation, AP Smartdesk), touchless Supplier Risk Radar and patented Auto Class spend classification, directly answering the buyer's top-down AI mandate ('you're giving us our pitch back'). The gap is proof over pitch: the buyer raised accuracy and AI-governance concerns that need concrete, Ashley-specific validation, and the value has not been quantified.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 8.0 / Strong and the calls corroborate it strongly rather than contradict: AI is the buyer's explicit, top-down driver ('the biggest push from upper management has been AI... emphasis on the AI'), the scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI and ANA, and the buyer probed Teams-based intake and AI accuracy. Fit is genuine, not over-rated.

items.detail: The AI mandate comes from unnamed upper management and no executive sponsor has been named or met; approvals run through a leadership layer Zycus cannot see.
The 25 Sep close lands inside the buyer's own final-vendor window with no room for proposal, negotiation, MSA/SOW, legal or InfoSec; a 45-60 day slip is likely.
items.source: Avoma intro call 8 May 2026; SF CloseDate history 12 May 2026

items.detail: Single-threaded to mid-level indirect procurement; the direct-side leaders and the executive sponsor are not engaged, and the senior contact Matthew Roby has not been reached.

items.detail: The platform must integrate with AS/400 ERP and coexist with Maximo (kept for work-order demand, not purchasing); integration depth is a scored criterion not yet proven.
Zycus could not price the RFP because the buyer never returned the user-count spreadsheet requested 15 May and chased 26 May; commercial questions remain unanswered.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: strong two-way demo 8 May, then Zycus-side outreach only (27 May submission, 17 Jun Next Step) with no buyer reply for 40 days as the shortlist decision comes due.
items: Buyer last responded 27 May; the user counts and commercial answers Zycus needs are unreturned.
Rep updated Next Step 17 Jun (rep-side touch), no buyer reply.
Strong positive AI signal on 8 May keeps the deal live despite the silence.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A live, AI-hungry S2P RFP where Zycus demoed strongly on 8 May, but the deal is single-threaded to mid-level procurement with no named executive buyer and the buyer has gone quiet for 40 days without returning the user counts needed to price; the win depends on re-igniting Ashley Frantz and reaching the AI sponsor before the final-two cut.

win_position.text: Genuinely competitive but early: Zycus is in a live multi-vendor RFP with a strong on-call AI reaction ('you're giving us our pitch back') and exact-fit Phase 1 scope, but no shortlist confirmation yet.
The buyer's AI mandate is top-down and the demanded stack (Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, Supplier Risk Radar) is Zycus's core differentiation, giving a real wedge if a rival surfaces.
No economic buyer is named and no executive has joined a call; approvals run through an unseen leadership layer, so Zycus cannot confirm sponsorship for the AI agenda.
The buyer has not responded since 27 May and never returned the user counts, leaving Zycus unable to price and the shortlist decision drifting.

deal_momentum.text: Fast early motion: Amount lifted to $320K, stage moved to Formal Evaluation, and the close pulled into 2026, all within two weeks of creation.
The buyer named concrete dates on 8 May: final two in 6-8 weeks, single vendor thereafter, go-live June 2027.
Momentum has stalled: no forward stage movement in ~56 days and the only recent touches are rep-side (27 May submission, 17 Jun Next Step).

customer_commitment.text: The buyer invited Zycus into an in-flight RFP after the conference and confirmed this is a budgeted project with leadership engaged.
Commitment is one-sided right now: the buyer owes user counts and commercial answers but has been silent 40 days, and only procurement (not the sponsor) is engaged.

deal_risk.text: The 25 Sep close date is not credible against the buyer's own final-vendor-plus-contracting timeline; a 45-60 day slip is likely.
Single-threaded to mid-level indirect procurement with the senior contact Matthew Roby uncontacted and the executive AI sponsor unnamed.

champion.evidence: Ashley Frantz owns the evaluation but has not advocated or opened the sponsor; developing, not confirmed.

executive_buyer.evidence: No named EB; approvals via unnamed upper management, no exec on any call.

engagement.evidence: Buyer silent 40 days; only rep-side touches since 27 May.

timeline.evidence: Close 25 Sep lands inside the buyer's final-vendor window with no contracting room.

scope_change.to: Phased: Phase 1 AI spend analytics, sourcing, supplier onboarding and risk; Phase 2 full indirect procure-to-pay out of Maximo; CLM out of scope
scope_change.detail: Scope is phased by the buyer's design, not narrowed: amount rose from $120K to $320K on 30 Apr and full S2P intent stands. The only carve-out is contract lifecycle management, which legal keeps in its own tool (Intellidree) - a boundary, not a defensive cut.

critical_signals.text: The 8 May intro demo landed strongly on AI, with the buyer saying Zycus was 'giving us our pitch back', but no two-way engagement has happened since.
Zycus cannot price the RFP because Ashley Furniture never returned the user-count spreadsheet requested 15 May and chased 26 May, and commercial questions remain unanswered.
It is a real final-two RFP but no rival has been named in any conversation or field, so the shortlist must be discovered before it can be positioned against.
Direct-side leaders Paul Ringo and Tim Adams surfaced on 8 May and were receptive, but neither has been followed up and the senior procurement contact Matthew Roby remains uncontacted.
The buyer scoped CLM out (legal keeps Intellidree) and requires coexistence with AS/400 and Maximo, shifting the fit test toward integration depth.

customer_preference.evidence: Strong positive AI reaction on 8 May ('you're giving us our pitch back') and Zycus pulled into the RFP after the conference, but no explicit lean over any rival and no Zycus-specific commercial next step requested.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative AI/efficiency pain articulated ('a lot of manual intervention... modernize our daily execution') but no savings %, spend-under-management, or ROI number stated.
